items.text:
NOTICE: Spring Course Evaluations — Deadline May 15

All undergraduate students must complete course evaluations for every enrolled class by 11:59 PM on Friday, May 15. Evaluations are available through the student portal under the Spring Semester tab.

Submission Guidelines:
• Evaluations must be completed for each individual course; partial submissions will not be saved.
• Students who complete all evaluations by May 10 will receive a $5.00 credit at the campus dining center.
• Once submitted, evaluations cannot be edited or withdrawn.
• A confirmation email will be sent within 30 minutes of submission.

Late submissions cannot be accepted and may delay the release of final spring grades. Students experiencing technical difficulties should contact the Registrar's Office at registrar@university.edu or visit Room 112 of Anderson Hall between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM on weekdays.

Professor responses to evaluation comments will be made available to students by June 5.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what do students who finish all evaluations by May 10 receive?

options.B: A $5.00 credit at the campus dining center
options.C: A waiver of one library overdue fee
options.D: A guaranteed registration slot for fall

questions.explanation: The notice states students who complete all evaluations by May 10 will receive a $5.00 credit at the campus dining center.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the notice?

options.A: To inform students of evaluation rules and deadlines
options.B: To announce a celebration for graduating seniors
options.C: To request volunteer help for the Registrar's Office
options.D: To advertise a new dining center menu launch

questions.explanation: The notice mainly informs students of when, how, and why they must complete course evaluations.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student whose submission fails on the evening of May 15?

options.A: The student can resubmit the evaluation in June
options.B: The student will receive a refund of all tuition fees
options.C: The student's final grades may be released after others
options.D: The student will be excused from the next term's evaluations

questions.explanation: The notice warns that late submissions cannot be accepted and may delay the release of final spring grades.

items.text:
GROVER LIBRARY — STUDY ROOM RESERVATION SCHEDULE

Location: Second Floor, Grover Library
Reservation portal: study.grover.edu

Available Hours by Day:
• Monday — Friday: 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM
• Saturday: 10:00 AM to 8:00 PM
• Sunday: 1:00 PM to 9:00 PM

Reservation Rules:
• Each student may reserve up to two hours per day.
• Reservations may be made up to seven days in advance through the portal.
• Rooms must be claimed within 15 minutes of the start time, or the reservation will be released.
• Group rooms (capacity 6 or more) require at least two registered users.
• Cancellations made less than two hours before the start time count as a no-show.
• Three no-shows in one month suspend reservation privileges for two weeks.

Quiet study desks on the first floor are always available without reservation. Questions may be directed to the Help Desk at extension 3120 between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM.

format_metadata.title: Grover Library — Study Room Reservation Schedule

questions.stem: According to the schedule, when does the library open on Sunday?
questions.explanation: The schedule lists Sunday hours as 1:00 PM to 9:00 PM.

questions.stem: According to the schedule, what happens if a reservation is not claimed in time?

options.A: It is held for the entire reserved period
options.B: It is transferred to a different room
options.C: It is released after a 15-minute grace period
options.D: It is rescheduled for the following day

questions.explanation: The schedule states rooms must be claimed within 15 minutes of the start time or the reservation will be released.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who misses three reservations in one month?

options.A: The student must pay a fee at the Help Desk
options.B: The student loses booking access for two weeks
options.C: The student is restricted to first-floor study desks for life
options.D: The student must complete an online training module

questions.explanation: The schedule states three no-shows in one month suspend reservation privileges for two weeks.
